Amba hɔdonno wɔɔ a hɩ m, nyɩnta k'a ʊ, an laaka da k'a nyɩ bɩ yɩrɛ dɔmɩm dɔ bɩ ma.
translated: The people who live in the village of Hɩrɔɔdɩ are very kind, they always help each other, and they share everything they have. They also respect the elders of Betelehɛm and listen to their advice, because they know that following the elders' guidance brings peace and prosperity to the whole community. When the village faces a problem, they gather together, discuss it, and work hard to solve it for the good of everyone.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: K'a dɩga bɛnnɔ ma: ŋ bɩr hɔ duure y, bɩ ŋ bɩr hɔ darɛ y, ŋ bɩr hɔ zarm sɩ ʊ y, b'awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ yɩ ŋ tʊrm! Awɔɔ n lɛ da ŋ nɩ gallɛm hɔka ra?
translated: When I arrive home: I will eat rice, I will drink water, I will rest a bit, then I will go to Z's house and stay there for a while! Will you come with me later?
